commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=86171267c728200fd079737014b13f6ec5dca7d7)
commands.review_summary:
The branch forwards the configured provider id into the Ollama memory embedding adapter and Gateway embeddings adapter path, with a mocked regression test for custom Ollama baseUrl resolution.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +28. Total +30 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level, without running a live Ollama server: current main passes the custom id into memory-core but the Ollama adapter overwrites it, and the Gateway memory-adapter path omits providerId. I did not run tests because this review is read-only and local Vitest can create artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs author or maintainer follow-up for the cache identity finding plus contributor-side real behavior proof; it is not a safe automated repair lane because the proof must come from the contributor's setup.

target.title: fix(ollama): preserve custom provider id through memory embedding adapter (#96742)
target.body:
## Summary

When a custom Ollama provider is defined under `models.providers` with `api: "ollama"` and a custom `baseUrl`, and used as `memorySearch.provider`, the memory engine resolves the custom provider ID back to the base `ollama` plugin's default baseUrl instead of the custom one.

**Root cause**: In `ollamaMemoryEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create()`, the `provider` parameter is hardcoded to `"ollama"`, overwriting the actual custom provider ID (e.g., `"ollama-proxy"`) that the caller correctly passes through. Downstream, `resolveConfiguredProvider()` uses `options.provider` to look up the provider config — since it always sees `"ollama"`, it finds the base plugin config (no custom baseUrl) instead of the user's custom provider config.

**Two-layer fix**:
1. `extensions/ollama/src/memory-embedding-adapter.ts`: Replace hardcoded `provider: "ollama"` with `provider: options.provider ?? "ollama"` (same pattern as `openAiMemoryEmbeddingProviderAdapter`)
2. `src/gateway/embeddings-http.ts`: Add `provider: providerId` to `createWithAdapter`'s `adapter.create()` call, matching `createWithGenericAdapter` which already passes provider

Not modified: `resolveConfiguredProvider` fallback logic, cacheKeyData, runtime id labels, other embedding adapters (bedrock, openai-compatible).

## Real Behavior Proof

**Environment**: Vitest unit tests with mocked HTTP fetch.

**Proof scripts**:
- `extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.test.ts` — test "passes custom provider ID through adapter to resolve custom baseUrl (#96742)"
- Existing test "uses custom Ollama provider config and strips that provider prefix" (now also implicitly validates the adapter path)

**Execution** (all 59 tests across 3 test files pass):
```
✓ |extension-providers| ollama embedding provider: passes custom provider ID through adapter to resolve custom baseUrl (#96742) 134ms
```

**Negative Control** (before fix):
- Adapter called with `provider: "ollama-proxy"`, config has `ollama-proxy: { baseUrl: "https://ollama-proxy.home.lab" }`
- Hardcoded `provider: "ollama"` → `resolveConfiguredProvider()` returns `providers.ollama` (base plugin, no custom baseUrl)
- `resolveOllamaEmbeddingBaseUrl()` falls back to `http://localhost:11434`

**After fix**:
- Adapter called with `provider: "ollama-proxy"`, config has `ollama-proxy: { baseUrl: "https://ollama-proxy.home.lab" }`
- `resolvedProvider = "ollama-proxy"` → `resolveConfiguredProvider("ollama-proxy")` returns `providers["ollama-proxy"]` (custom config with baseUrl)
- `resolveOllamaEmbeddingBaseUrl()` reads custom `baseUrl` → `https://ollama-proxy.home.lab/api/embed`
- Verified: `expectEmbeddingFetch(fetchMock, "https://ollama-proxy.home.lab/api/embed", ...)` ✅

**Untested**: Real ollama server integration (requires running ollama instance with custom baseUrl).

## Regression Test Plan

```
$ npx vitest run extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.test.ts
✓ 26 passed (26)

$ npx vitest run src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts
✓ 28 passed (28)

$ npx vitest run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts
✓ 7 passed (7)
```

All 61 tests pass. No other files modified. No debug output or console.log residues.

## Root Cause

In `ollamaMemoryEmbeddingProviderAdapter.create()` (`extensions/ollama/src/memory-embedding-adapter.ts:16`), `provider` is unconditionally set to `"ollama"` when calling `createOllamaEmbeddingProvider()`. This overwrites the actual custom provider ID (e.g., `"ollama-proxy"`) that the caller (`createEmbeddingProvider` in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.ts:247`) correctly passes through via `...options`. Downstream, `resolveConfiguredProvider()` (`extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.ts:148`) uses `options.provider` to look up the provider config in `models.providers`. Since it always sees `"ollama"`, it returns the base plugin config (which has no custom baseUrl), and `resolveOllamaEmbeddingBaseUrl()` falls back to the default `http://localhost:11434`. Only propagating the actual provider ID through the chain allows `resolveConfiguredProvider` to find the user's custom provider config with the correct baseUrl.

The same gap exists in the HTTP gateway path (`src/gateway/embeddings-http.ts:132`), where `createWithAdapter` omitted the `provider` parameter entirely (unlike `createWithGenericAdapter` which correctly passes it). With the adapter fix alone, omitting provider defaults to `"ollama"` which still works for the base case, but passing the correct provider is needed for the HTTP path to resolve custom configs.
target.author: maweibin
